mnemonics: Mini Batch: Middle Ground Memory - Faster than a walk (Batch), cheaper than a taxi (Stochastic).
keyPoints: A compromise between batch and stochastic gradient descent that uses a subset (mini-batch) of the training data for each update.
It reduces the variance of the parameter updates compared to SGD, leading to more stable convergence.
It is computationally more efficient than full batch GD and is the standard algorithm for training deep neural networks.
commonMistakes: Choosing a mini-batch size that is too small (high variance, noisy updates) or too large (slow, loses benefit over full batch).
Not shuffling the training data before creating mini-batches, which can introduce bias and harm convergence.
